title: Bardo Thodol: Liberation Through Hearing in the Intermediate State
description: The Tibetan Book of the Dead: instructions for consciousness navigating the states between death and rebirth.
introduction: Known in the West as the Tibetan Book of the Dead, the Bardo Thodol is a guide for the consciousness of the recently deceased as it navigates the intermediate states (bardos) between death and rebirth. Attributed to the 8th-century master Padmasambhava and hidden as a terma (treasure text), it was rediscovered centuries later. The text describes encounters with peaceful and wrathful deities that are, in truth, projections of the mind itself.

translation.text: Now when the bardo of reality dawns upon me, I shall recognize all appearances as the dharmakaya.
This great self-arising luminosity has never been obscured by the darkness of ignorance.
